rationale.logic: p53 loss abolishes the G1/S checkpoint. The tumor then depends on the G2/M checkpoint (governed by WEE1, and upstream ATR-CHK1) to pause and repair DNA damage before mitosis. Inhibiting that checkpoint WHILE delivering DNA damage (radiation or alkylator) forces cells into mitosis with unrepaired damage -> mitotic catastrophe. This is synthetic lethality: selectively lethal in the p53-deficient background, relatively spared in normal (p53-intact) cells.
rationale.second_hit: TERT promoter C250T (V1) drives telomere replication stress, which further sensitizes to ATR inhibition.
rationale.penetration: Paxalisib is engineered brain-penetrant; adavosertib and several ATR inhibitors have documented CNS/tumor exposure.

primary_evidence.claim: WEE1 is overexpressed in GBM and is the major G2-checkpoint regulator; its inhibition + DNA damage causes mitotic catastrophe and radiosensitizes GBM in vivo
ATR inhibitors are synthetic-lethal in p53/ATM-deficient tumors
Adavosertib (WEE1i) + radiation feasible in newly diagnosed diffuse glioma (early-phase clinical)
Paxalisib Phase 2 in newly diagnosed GBM, unmethylated MGMT

recruiting_trials.name: Debio 0123 (WEE1 inhibitor) + temozolomide ± radiotherapy
recruiting_trials.fit: IDH-wildtype GBM / Grade III; recurrence-eligible; measurable disease by RANO
recruiting_trials.why: Direct test of the WEE1 arm of this exact strategy in IDH-wt GBM (matches the tumor's IDH status).

recruiting_trials.name: GBM AGILE (adaptive platform; paxalisib is an arm)
recruiting_trials.fit: Dedicated RECURRENT GBM inclusion arm; KPS>=60; IDH-wt
recruiting_trials.sites: 63 sites — USA, Canada, France, Germany, Australia, Switzerland
recruiting_trials.why: Largest access route to paxalisib for RECURRENT GBM; adaptive design rotates in the best regimens.

recruiting_trials.name: 5G-PEARL: paxalisib + temozolomide
recruiting_trials.why: Paxalisib+TMZ combination; single UK site (access depends on geography).

safety_sequencing: Myelosuppression is the shared dose-limiting toxicity of WEE1/ATR inhibitors + alkylators — sequencing and dose de-escalation matter.
WEE1/ATR inhibitors are typically given INTERMITTENTLY around the DNA-damage source (RT/TMZ), not continuously — the timing is the therapy.
Paxalisib toxicities: hyperglycemia (on-target PI3K), oral mucositis — relevant if combining with metformin (which helps glycemia).
This core is access-limited: the drugs are largely trial-gated. The realistic route is a trial (above) or off-label onc supervision.
